prompt:
Add a short **Product context** section at the top of `CLAUDE.md` (right after the title `# Claude Code Rules for Flux`, before `## Git & GitHub`). This documents the product framing in the repo so future Claude sessions and contributors pick it up automatically.

**Why now:** the user clarified that flux is not a standalone workouts app — it's one of three touchpoints (flux/vibe/balance) into a single AI wellness coach. That framing should be visible in CLAUDE.md so future decisions about flux (scope, features, integration points) get made with the coach in mind, not against flux as an isolated workouts app.

**Add this section verbatim** (adjust wording lightly if it improves clarity, but keep it tight — five-ish lines):

```md
## Product context

Flux is the **workouts** surface of a three-app wellness toolset driven by an AI coach. The siblings are **vibe** (meditation, native Android, separate repo) and **balance** (check-ins / tracking, `gunk-dev/balance`, Capacitor + React, syncs via Google Sheets).

The coach generates programs, reviews progress, adapts to mood / sickness / stress signals from check-ins, and schedules reminders. Flux is one of its expressions — design and architecture decisions should be evaluated against the coaching relationship, not against flux as a standalone app.
```

**Do not** modify any other section of CLAUDE.md. **Do not** introduce wording about how the coach is implemented (where it runs, what it stores) — that's unsettled; keep this purely about the product framing.

**PR title:** "docs: document product context — flux as workouts surface of wellness suite"

**PR body:** one line — "Adds product framing to CLAUDE.md so future contributors and Claude sessions understand flux's role in the broader AI wellness coach product."

This is a tiny doc-only change; should be a one-commit PR.

**Out of scope:** any architectural changes; any wording about implementation details (backend vs in-app LLM, sync strategy, etc); editing README.md or any other doc files.
